meta_title: For Founders: user onboarding to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: User Onboarding to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Learn how to use onboarding flows to validate SaaS ideas and overcome creator block. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' trap is actually a validation failure. By building a high-friction user onboarding flow first, you force yourself to define the core value proposition. If you can't onboard a user to a specific benefit, you don't have a product idea worth building yet.
content.intro:
The blank screen is the graveyard of ambition. You’ve spent weeks oscillating between half-baked SaaS concepts, paralyzed by the fear that your next project will be another silent launch. The tension of 'no project ideas' isn't a lack of creativity; it's a lack of structural focus.

True founders don't start with code; they start with the user's journey. By flipping the script and building a modular onboarding flow, you stop guessing what to build and start observing what users actually need. Let’s turn your paralysis into a profitable, validated product architecture.

steps.title: Define the Value Pivot
steps.description: Identify the one problem your onboarding will solve. We aren't building a full app; we are building a 'concierge' flow that validates intent.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a schema for a 3-step SaaS onboarding flow that captures user pain points and maps them to a hypothetical solution.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate initial onboarding schema and user flow state machine'

steps.title: Build the Skeleton
steps.description: Use Antigravity to scaffold the core React/Next.js components. Keep it lean; focus on state management for the user progress.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular Next.js onboarding component structure using Tailwind CSS for a professional, minimalist look.

steps.title: Integrate Intent Tracking
steps.description: Every click in your onboarding is data. We need to log these interactions to see where users drop off.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add server-side logging to the onboarding state transitions to track user intent and drop-off points.

steps.title: Implement the 'Aha!' Moment
steps.description: Ensure the final step of the onboarding delivers instant value, even if the backend is just a mock service.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a final onboarding screen that displays a summary of the user's input as a 'custom roadmap' or 'result'.

steps.title: Deployment for Validation
steps.description: Deploy to a staging environment and share with 10 potential users to see if they complete the flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure a Vercel deployment script to push the onboarding prototype to a live preview URL.

pro_tips: Never ask for an email until the user has received at least one 'aha' moment in the flow.
Use 'progressive disclosure'—only show the next input field after the previous one is successfully validated.
If your onboarding flow takes longer than 60 seconds, you are asking for too much information.
Use the 'Concierge MVP' approach: manually fulfill the promise made in the onboarding flow to test if people actually want it.
Track the 'Time-to-Value' (TTV) metric strictly; if TTV exceeds 2 minutes, your onboarding is killing your conversion.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding flow is live, share the link in 3 niche communities. Analyze the drop-off data in your analytics dashboard, iterate on the messaging, and observe which user pain point gets the highest completion rate. That is your product idea.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for yourself and start building for your customers. Use this onboarding-first approach to validate your next million-dollar idea before writing a single line of backend logic. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
